name: Michigan Food Assistance Program (FAP)
description: Michigan's SNAP program providing food assistance via the Bridge Card to eligible low-income individuals and families to purchase groceries.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income and asset guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Generally up to 200% of the federal poverty level for gross income; net income must be at or below 100% FPL
hours: Local MDHHS offices: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: MI Bridges
description: Michigan's online portal for applying for and managing public assistance benefits including food assistance, healthcare, cash assistance, and child care.
eligibilitySummary: Any Michigan resident can create an account to apply for benefits or find community resources
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program applied for
hours: Online portal available 24/7

name: Healthy Michigan Plan
description: Michigan's expanded Medicaid program providing health coverage to low-income adults aged 19-64 who do not qualify for other Medicaid programs.
eligibilitySummary: Michigan residents aged 19-64 with income at or below 133% of the federal poverty level who are not eligible for or enrolled in Medicare
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 133% FPL (approximately $20,120 for an individual in 2025)

name: MIChild
description: Health care coverage program for uninsured children of Michigan working families, offering comprehensive benefits for a low monthly premium.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under age 19 in families with income up to 217% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 217% FPL; $10/month per family premium

name: Michigan Unemployment Insurance Agency (UIA)
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who become unemployed through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period; weekly benefit amount varies
hours: Phone: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Michigan Web Account Manager (MiWAM)
description: Online system for managing unemployment insurance claims, checking benefit payments, certifying for benefits, and updating personal information.
eligibilitySummary: Current and former unemployment insurance claimants
hours: Online portal available 24/7; phone support Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Michigan State Housing Development Authority (MSHDA)
description: Statewide housing authority providing rental assistance through the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program, homeownership assistance, and affordable housing resources across all 83 Michigan counties.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income individuals and families; head of household must be at least 18; must pass criminal background check
incomeLimitNotes: Household income cannot exceed HUD-established income limits, which vary by county and family size
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: MSHDA Housing Choice Voucher Program
description: Federal rental subsidy program administered by MSHDA that helps very low-income families afford decent, safe housing in the private market.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals; income must not exceed HUD limits
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income; 75% of vouchers must go to families at or below 30% AMI
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Michigan Legal Help
description: Statewide website with tools, information, and self-help resources to help people understand and manage their civil legal problems without an attorney.
eligibilitySummary: Any person with a Michigan civil legal issue
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: Legal Aid of Western Michigan
description: Provides free civil legal advocacy and representation to low-income people and families across western Michigan in areas including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits.
address: Cornerstone Building, 1 W. Michigan Ave, Suite 200, Battle Creek, MI 49017
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of western Michigan counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally must be at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
hours: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM

name: Legal Aid and Defender Association (LAD)
description: The oldest provider of free civil legal assistance in Michigan, representing low- and moderate-income residents of Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties in civil legal matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low- and moderate-income residents of Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility guidelines apply
hours: Phone intake: Monday, Wednesday, 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM and 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM

name: Legal Services of Eastern Michigan (LSEM)
description: Provides free civil legal help to low-income residents in 14 Mid-Michigan counties including Genesee, Saginaw, Bay, and surrounding areas.
address: 436 S. Saginaw Street, Suite 101, Flint, MI 48502
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Arenac, Bay, Clare, Genesee, Gladwin, Gratiot, Huron, Isabella, Lapeer, Midland, Saginaw, Sanilac, St. Clair, and Tuscola counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally must be at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
hours: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Michigan Legal Services
description: Statewide legal aid organization providing free legal assistance to low-income Michigan residents in civil matters including public benefits, housing, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility guidelines apply

name: Pure Michigan Talent Connect
description: Michigan's official online labor exchange connecting job seekers with employers. Provides job search tools, career exploration, labor market information, and training resources.
eligibilitySummary: Any Michigan job seeker or employer
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: Michigan Works! Association
description: Statewide network of 16 Michigan Works! Agencies providing workforce development services including job training, career counseling, resume assistance, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: Any Michigan job seeker or employer; additional training programs may have income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: WIOA-funded training programs may require income eligibility
hours: Varies by local Michigan Works! Service Center

name: Michigan Rehabilitation Services (MRS)
description: Statewide vocational rehabilitation program helping individuals with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and retain employment through counseling, training, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create a barrier to employment
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM at 35 offices statewide

name: State Emergency Relief (SER) Program
description: Provides immediate financial assistance to individuals and families facing emergencies that threaten health and safety, including help with utility shutoffs, home repairs, and relocation.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan residents facing emergency situations threatening health or safety
incomeLimitNotes: Income and asset limits apply; varies by type of emergency assistance
hours: Apply online via MI Bridges 24/7 or at local MDHHS offices Monday-Friday

name: Michigan Energy Assistance Program (MEAP)
description: Provides energy assistance to help low-income households pay heating and electric bills, funded through federal LIHEAP and state funds.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan households needing help with energy bills
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 150% of the federal poverty level

name: MDHHS Energy and Weatherization Assistance
description: Provides energy-related emergency services and weatherization improvements to help low-income households reduce energy costs and improve home energy efficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan households; priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level for weatherization

name: The Heat and Warmth Fund (THAW)
description: Nonprofit organization providing emergency utility bill payment assistance and promoting energy efficiency education to prevent utility shutoffs for Michigan residents.
eligibilitySummary: Michigan residents facing utility shutoff or unable to pay energy bills
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility guidelines apply; varies by funding source

name: Michigan 2-1-1
description: Free, confidential statewide service connecting people with local community-based organizations offering thousands of programs and services including food, housing, utility assistance, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: Any Michigan resident seeking information about available community services
hours: 24/7 phone and online access; text your zip code to 898-211
languages: English, Spanish, and interpretation services for 150+ languages

name: Michigan Crisis and Access Line (MiCAL)
description: Statewide 24/7 crisis line for mental health, substance use, and suicidal crisis support. Also serves as the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline call center for Michigan.
eligibilitySummary: Any person in Michigan experiencing a mental health, substance use, or suicidal crisis

name: Gleaners Community Food Bank
description: Regional food bank serving southeast Michigan, operating five distribution centers across Wayne, Oakland, Macomb, Livingston, and Monroe counties providing food to hundreds of partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Any individual or family in need of food assistance in southeast Michigan
hours: Varies by distribution site

name: Food Bank of Eastern Michigan
description: Regional food bank serving 22 counties in eastern Michigan, distributing food to partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, soup kitchens, and schools.
eligibilitySummary: Any individual or family in need of food assistance in the 22-county service area of eastern Michigan
hours: Varies by distribution site

name: Feeding America West Michigan
description: Regional food bank serving 40 counties in western Michigan and the Upper Peninsula, distributing food through a network of partner agencies.
address: 864 West River Center Dr NE, Comstock Park, MI 49321
eligibilitySummary: Any individual or family in need of food assistance in western Michigan and the Upper Peninsula
hours: Varies by distribution site

name: Food Bank Council of Michigan
description: Statewide coalition of Michigan's regional food banks coordinating hunger relief efforts and advocating on behalf of the hunger relief network.
eligibilitySummary: Connects individuals to their regional food bank and local food pantries

name: Family Independence Program (FIP)
description: Michigan's TANF cash assistance program providing temporary financial help to families with dependent children while they work toward self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Families with dependent children meeting income and asset limits; must participate in work activities
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size. Asset limit of $3,000 for most families
hours: Apply online via MI Bridges 24/7 or at local MDHHS offices Monday-Friday

name: State Disability Assistance (SDA)
description: Provides cash assistance to eligible disabled adults and caretakers of disabled individuals who are not receiving other cash assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Disabled adults, caretakers of disabled persons, or persons age 65 or older not receiving SSI or FIP
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; asset limit applies
hours: Apply online via MI Bridges 24/7 or at local MDHHS offices Monday-Friday

name: Michigan WIC Program
description: Federally-funded supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health referrals to eligible pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children up to age 5 who are at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level
hours: Varies by local clinic

name: Child Development and Care (CDC) Program
description: Michigan's child care subsidy program helping low-income working families access affordable, quality child care and after-school programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families who need child care assistance while working, attending school, or in job training
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility limits vary by family size; families may need to pay a copay based on income
hours: Phone: Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM - 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM - 4:30 PM

name: Great Start Readiness Program (GSRP)
description: Michigan's free state-funded preschool program for eligible four-year-old children, providing high-quality early childhood education to prepare children for kindergarten.
eligibilitySummary: Four-year-old children from families meeting income and other risk factor criteria
incomeLimitNotes: Priority given to families at or below 250% of the federal poverty level; other risk factors considered
hours: Varies by program site; typically follows school year schedule

name: Detroit Department of Transportation (DDOT)
description: Public transit system serving the City of Detroit, Highland Park, and Hamtramck with fixed-route bus service. Offers reduced fares for seniors, youth, and riders with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all riders; reduced fares available for seniors 65+, youth 6-18, Medicare card holders, and persons with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Standard 4-hour pass $2.00; reduced fare $0.50
hours: Service hours vary by route; generally 4:00 AM - 1:00 AM

name: SMART (Suburban Mobility Authority for Regional Transportation)
description: Regional public transit system serving the suburban Detroit metro area with fixed-route, connector, and paratransit services. Offers reduced fares and seamless transfers with DDOT.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all riders; reduced fares for seniors 65+, youth 6-18, and persons with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Standard 4-hour pass $2.00; reduced fare $0.50; 31-day regional pass $70 (reduced $29)
hours: Service hours vary by route; customer service Monday-Friday

name: Michigan Veterans Affairs Agency (MVAA)
description: Central state agency providing support, care, advocacy, and connection to services for Michigan veterans and their families, including benefits assistance and the Michigan Veteran Resource Service Center.
address: 3423 N. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd, Bldg 32, Lansing, MI 48906
eligibilitySummary: Michigan veterans, service members, and their families
hours: Michigan Veteran Resource Service Center available during business hours

name: Wayne County Health, Human & Veterans Services
description: Wayne County department providing health promotion, human services, and veterans assistance programs to Wayne County residents including Detroit.
eligibilitySummary: Wayne County residents, including City of Detroit
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: MDHHS Wayne County Office
description: Local Michigan Department of Health and Human Services office serving Wayne County residents with applications and case management for cash assistance, food assistance, Medicaid, child care, and emergency relief.
eligibilitySummary: Wayne County residents applying for or receiving MDHHS benefits
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Wayne Metropolitan Community Action Agency
description: Nonprofit serving Wayne County with over 100 programs in housing, family stability, and economic opportunity including emergency assistance, financial coaching, and housing counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Low- and moderate-income residents of Wayne County
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally serves low- to moderate-income households
hours: Connect Center: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Saturday, 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM

name: Detroit at Work
description: City of Detroit's workforce development initiative providing a single entry point for job seekers and employers, with career centers offering job search, training, and career pathway guidance.
eligibilitySummary: Detroit residents seeking employment or training; some programs prioritize Detroit residents
hours: Career Centers: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Michigan Community Action
description: Statewide association of 27 Community Action Agencies helping low-income residents move from poverty to self-sufficiency through programs including energy assistance, weatherization, housing, and emergency services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan residents; specific eligibility varies by program and local agency
incomeLimitNotes: Generally serves households at or below 200% of the federal poverty level
hours: Varies by local Community Action Agency

name: Michigan Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Federally-funded program providing free home energy improvements to low-income households to reduce energy costs and improve comfort and safety, administered through local Community Action Agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Michigan homeowners and renters; priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with children under 6
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level
hours: Contact local Community Action Agency for hours

name: Michigan Disability Resources
description: State portal connecting people with disabilities to information about state programs, benefits, employment, housing, and community resources across Michigan.
eligibilitySummary: Michigan residents with disabilities and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: LEO - Michigan Workforce Development
description: Michigan Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity's workforce development division overseeing statewide employment and training programs, apprenticeships, and career services.
eligibilitySummary: Michigan job seekers and employers seeking workforce development assistance
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
